def: Formed of a mixture of horny and calcareous materials, as someshells and corals.
Making a false appearance; unreal; false; as, pretended friend.-- Pre*tend"ed*ly, adv.
Placed alone or apart, as if on an island; severed, as anisland. [R.] "In the sea of life enisled." M. Arnold.
Disposed to dispute; inclined to cavil or to reason inopposition; as, a disputative temper. I. Watts.
Of the nature of, or like, copper; brassy. [R.]
The state of a parent; the office or character of a parent.
Bound by, or as by, a spell.
